target.name: Wiliot
target.founder: Tal Tamir, Yaron Elboim, Alon Yehezkely

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $230M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: DEEPX
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series C), post-money $529M

data.narrative:
Wiliot is a Series C-stage semiconductor company that has raised $230M to date. Valuation analysis relies on a peer set of 4 private companies in the chips and semiconductors sector at same or adjacent stages—SambaNova Systems ($5.0B, Series D), Tenstorrent ($2.7B, Series D), Positron AI ($1.0B, Series B), and DEEPX ($529M, Series C)—alongside reference to 3 public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement, yielding $391M. Peer-median analysis across the 4-company set produces $1.9B, with a range spanning $529M to $5.0B depending on stage proximity and business maturity. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to lack of disclosed revenue data, a common constraint for private companies.

The combined analysis supports a valuation range of $332M (low) to $2.1B (high), with a median of $1.1B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around whether Wiliot's market position and trajectory align with earlier-stage peers like DEEPX or more mature Series D entrants. Key unknowns include revenue run-rate, customer concentration, and path to profitability or exit, all of which would materially narrow this range.
